question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: init() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user flood all configured Hedera nodes with connection-refusing responses during the initial Flux.interval() validation cycle, causing nodes CopyOnWriteArrayList to remain permanently empty and NodeSupplier.get() to throw IllegalArgumentException on every publish attempt, resulting in a complete denial-of-service for the monitor's transaction publishing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: refresh() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user partition the network between the monitor and the mirror node REST API so that getAddressBook() returns an empty Flux, and the fallback monitorProperties.getNetwork().getNodes() list is also unreachable, triggering the Flux.error(new IllegalArgumentException('Nodes must not be empty')) path and causing the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1L))) to spin indefinitely, exhausting scheduler threads?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validateNode() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained network partition to all Hedera consensus nodes so that every validateNode() call catches TimeoutException, removes the node from the nodes list via nodes.remove(node), and eventually empties the CopyOnWriteArrayList, causing the entire publish flow to fail with no automatic recovery until the next scheduled validation frequency interval (default 1 day)?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: get() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the periodic node validation (which removes nodes via nodes.remove()) and the counter.getAndIncrement() % nodes.size() calculation in get(), where nodes.size() drops to zero between the isEmpty() check and the modulo operation, causing an ArithmeticException (division by zero) that propagates up through the publish pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish() bean in MonitorConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes the Flux.generate() publish pipeline to emit errors faster than onErrorContinue() can suppress them, causing the Schedulers.newParallel('publisher') thread pool to saturate and the sequential() operator to back-pressure indefinitely, effectively freezing the entire monitor application?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth() in SubscriberHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user partition the network between the monitor and the mirror node REST API so that getNetworkStakeStatusCode() always times out after 5 seconds, causing the onErrorResume handler to classify the TimeoutException as Status.DOWN, which then propagates to the cluster health metric CLUSTER_UP being set to 0, triggering false-positive alerting and masking real network partition events?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: health() in ImporterLagHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user partition the network between the monitor and the Prometheus endpoint so that prometheusClient.query() throws an exception, causing the catch block to return up() (Health.up()) regardless of actual cluster state, meaning a real network partition causing importer lag is silently masked and never reported as DOWN?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildLagQuery() in ImporterLagHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a Prometheus-scraped metric label inject a malicious cluster regex value into the PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY via the clusterRegex property (formatted into the PromQL string without sanitization), causing the query to match unintended clusters or produce a PromQL injection that returns manipulated lag values, leading to incorrect DOWN/UP health decisions during a real network partition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: clientSubscribe() in GrpcSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition to the gRPC mirror node endpoint so that the Retry.backoff(retry.getMaxAttempts(), retry.getMinBackoff()) retry loop exhausts all maxAttempts (default 16) and the doOnError handler logs the final error, after which the subscription silently terminates with no automatic restart, causing the monitor to report zero subscription rate and trigger false health DOWN status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: shouldRetry() in GrpcSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in gRPC status UNAVAILABLE being returned (not INVALID_ARGUMENT), so shouldRetry() returns true and the retry loop runs indefinitely up to Long.MAX_VALUE attempts in the NodeSupplier refresh() retrySpec, consuming all available scheduler threads and causing a thread starvation denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: clientSubscribe() in RestSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user partition the network so that the REST API consistently returns HTTP 404 for transaction lookups, causing shouldRetry() to return true and the Retry.backoff() loop to retry up to maxAttempts times per transaction, multiplied across all parallel publish threads, creating a retry storm that exhausts the WebClient connection pool and causes cascading failures across all REST subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes() in RestApiClient] Can an unprivileged external user partition the network so that the paginated getNodes() call (starting at /network/nodes?limit=25) retrieves partial results before the connection drops, leaving the AtomicReference next in an inconsistent state where it points to a non-null but unreachable URL, causing the Flux.repeat() to loop indefinitely and never complete the address book refresh?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: init() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the repeatWhen(repeat -> repeat.filter(l -> nodes.isEmpty())) logic in NodeSupplier.init() by causing intermittent network partitions that repeatedly empty the nodes list, triggering continuous re-validation cycles that each spawn new parallel scheduler threads via Schedulers.newParallel('validator'), leading to unbounded thread creation and eventual JVM OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient() in NodeSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition during the TransferTransaction validation in validateNode(), where the transaction is submitted but the receipt query times out, leaving the Hedera SDK client in an inconsistent state where the client is closed via try-with-resources but the underlying gRPC channel remains open, causing resource leaks that accumulate over repeated validation cycles?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish() in TransactionPublisher] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes all Hedera nodes unreachable, so that the clients Flux (cached via .cache()) was built with a stale node list from before the partition, and since the cache is never invalidated, the monitor continues attempting to publish to unreachable endpoints indefinitely without ever refreshing the client pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients() in TransactionPublisher] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition during the initial getClients() call (which calls nodeSupplier.refresh()), so that the Flux<Client> clients cache is populated with zero clients, and subsequent calls to clients.elementAt(clientIndex) always throw IndexOutOfBoundsException, permanently breaking the publish pipeline until the application is restarted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe() in GrpcClientSDK] Can an unprivileged external user partition the network to the gRPC mirror node endpoint so that Client.forNetwork(Map.of()) followed by client.setMirrorNetwork() succeeds (no network call at construction time), but the actual subscription in subscribeToClient() immediately fails, and since the clients Flux is cached via .cache(), the broken clients are reused for all subsequent subscription attempts without ever being replaced?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeToClient() in GrpcClientSDK] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition mid-subscription so that the topicMessageQuery error handler calls sink.tryEmitError(throwable), but the doFinally handler calls subscriptionHandle.unsubscribe() on an already-errored subscription, causing an unhandled exception in the SDK that bypasses the Reactor error handling chain and propagates as an unhandled thread exception?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: health() in SubscriberHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that simultaneously drops both the REST network stake endpoint and all gRPC subscriptions, so that restNetworkStakeHealth() returns DOWN while subscribing() also returns DOWN, but the health() method's flatMap chain short-circuits on the first DOWN from restNetworkStakeHealth() and never evaluates the subscribing() path, causing the health status to be determined solely by the REST partition rather than the actual subscription state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publishing() in SubscriberHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that drops all Hedera node connections, causing all publish scenarios to have a rate of 0.0, so that publishing() returns getHealthForZeroRate() which returns DOWN (when failWhenInactive=true), even though the partition is external and the monitor itself is functioning correctly, causing false-positive DOWN health reports that trigger unnecessary incident responses?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetric() in PublishMetrics] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained network partition that generates a continuous stream of PublishException errors with unique node/scenario/status tag combinations, causing the submitTimers ConcurrentHashMap and handleTimers ConcurrentHashMap to grow without bound as new Timer instances are registered in the MeterRegistry for each unique tag set, eventually causing an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext() in SubscribeMetrics] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in a flood of SubscribeResponse objects with null publishedTimestamp values (as seen in GrpcClientSDK.toResponse() when Utility.getTimestamp() returns null), causing the latencyMetrics computeIfAbsent to never register timers for affected scenarios, silently dropping latency data and making the partition invisible in metrics dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscriptions() in SubscriberController] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call GET /api/v1/subscriber with various protocol and status query parameters during a network partition, causing the mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() Flux to be subscribed to many times concurrently, and since the underlying subscriptions Flux is cached, each concurrent request holds a reference preventing garbage collection of scenario state objects, causing memory pressure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscription() in SubscriberController] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/subscriber/{name}/{id} with an arbitrary name and id during a network partition where all subscriptions are in COMPLETED state, causing the subscriptions.filter(s -> s.getId() == id).last() call to throw NoSuchElementException which is caught by the @ExceptionHandler and returns HTTP 404, but the repeated calls during partition recovery cause the reactive chain to be re-evaluated on every request, creating unnecessary load?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: next() in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes all nodes unreachable, so that the publish() bean's Flux.generate() loop calls transactionGenerator.next(0) at maximum speed (no back-pressure from the network), causing the remaining AtomicLong to decrement rapidly toward zero and triggering ScenarioException('Reached publish limit') prematurely, permanently stopping the publish scenario even after the partition is resolved?
